tldr: Move from Nix-powered full dev environments (Devenv) to Hermit's lightweight per-project hermetic CLI tool pinning, accepting a narrower scope in exchange for less Nix complexity.
why_switch: Avoid Nix learning curve and macOS/Linux-only friction
Simpler YAML-based tool pinning vs devenv.nix
Smaller surface area: just CLI tools, no full environment graph
Easier onboarding for teams uncomfortable with Nix errors
Cash App-backed maintenance for the core tool manager
what_to_export: List of language toolchains pinned in devenv.nix (Go, Node, Python versions)
List of CLI utilities (ripgrep, jq, kubectl, etc.) installed via Devenv
Service definitions (Postgres, Redis, Caddy) — note these will NOT migrate
Custom scripts defined under scripts in devenv.nix
CI configuration that invokes devenv shell
export_method: There is no automated exporter. Manually inventory devenv.nix: extract languages.<name>.enable + version, packages list, scripts, and env vars into a plain list. Treat services (Postgres/Redis) as a separate migration concern since Hermit does not orchestrate services — consult official Hermit docs for current scope.
import_method: Initialize Hermit in the repo root following the official Hermit getting-started guide (typically a curl-based bootstrap). Use hermit install <package>@<version> for each CLI tool you previously had in devenv.nix. Commit the resulting bin/ directory and activation scripts. Replace devenv shell invocations in CI with sourcing the Hermit activation script. Verify exact commands and current package catalog against the official Hermit docs.

concept_mapping.note: One-to-one for tools present in Hermit's catalog; otherwise no equivalent
Hermit does not orchestrate services — use Docker Compose or similar separately
Hermit has no script runner; move to project tooling of choice
Hermit provides its own activation; consult docs for direnv coexistence
Install pre-commit via Hermit then configure normally

gotchas: Hermit does not replace service orchestration — plan a parallel solution before cutover
Tool catalog is smaller than nixpkgs; some packages may be unavailable
Reproducibility model differs: Hermit pins binaries, Devenv pins via Nix store hashes
Windows still relies on WSL — same constraint as Devenv
Custom scripts from devenv.nix need a new home (Make, just, npm scripts)
Verify all tool names and commands against the current official Hermit documentation

email.subject: Proposal: replace Devenv with Hermit for our toolchain pinning
email.body:
Hi team,

I want to propose moving our dev tooling from Devenv to Hermit. Devenv has served us well for pinned toolchains, but the Nix layer adds onboarding friction and we don't actually use the service-orchestration features much.

Hermit gives us per-project hermetic CLI installs without Nix. Trade-offs: no service orchestration, smaller package catalog. We'd keep services in Docker Compose. Estimated effort 4-12 hours plus team ramp.

Worth a spike?

Thanks
